qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Duplicate multipart parameters allow upload-filter bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets duplicate-parameter ambiguity and mixed-parser upload-filter bypass, while the advisory describes catastrophic regex backtracking in header parsing that causes denial of service.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's regex-based ReDoS root cause. The only real finding in the run is a separate duplicate-parameter parsing ambiguity that can bypass upload filters in a mixed-parser application.
evidence: GHSA-jg4p-7fhp-p32p describes denial of service from crafted Content-Type and Content-Disposition header values that trigger catastrophic backtracking in three regular expressions, fixed by tightening those regexes in @hapi/content 6.0.1.

duplicate_parameter_upload_filter_bypass/vulnerability_report.json instead describes silent acceptance of duplicate parameters and inconsistent first-match versus last-write parsing behavior in Content.type() and Content.disposition().

The finding's PoC proves an application-level upload-filter bypass by combining a first-occurrence filename check with Content.disposition() returning the last filename value; it does not demonstrate regex backtracking, process hangs, or other advisory-matching DoS behavior.
